text:
Yoshi and the Mysterious Book icons added to Nintendo Switch Online

May 20: Yoshi and the Mysterious Book is here, and to celebrate, new icons are now available on Nintendo Switch Online. Icons will be rotated weekly over the next month or so. You can grab designs for characters, backgrounds, and frames. Characters cost 10 Platinum Points each on My Nintendo. For backgrounds and frames, they’re 5 Platinum Points each. These...

text:
Nintendo reportedly planning Switch 2 revision with replaceable battery for Europe [update: confirmed]

March 20: According to a report from Japanese outlet Nikkei, Nintendo will release an updated Switch 2 model that features a replaceable battery for the European market. The move would come in accordance with European Union legislation so that consumers can easily replace the battery themselves. While this is targeted at the European market, similar policies could take place in...

text:

Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game has been delayed, though thankfully it’ll only be a few extra weeks. Last we heard, the title was planned for July 2, 2026 on Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch. text:
Disney Dreamlight Valley “A Hero’s Journey” update announced [update: trailer]

May 26: Disney Dreamlight Valley is still pumping out updates, and the latest one has been revealed – titled “A Hero’s Journey”. Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch players can access it on June 3, 2026. Following yesterday’s announcement of Rayman Legends Retold, some fans might be wondering if we could ever see a remake of Rayman 1. Unfortunately, that seems unlikely to happen. text:

Curveball Games and Top Hat Studios have announced that Under the Island, a Zelda-like adventure that launched digitally earlier this year, will be receiving a physical release on Nintendo Switch. text:

Colorbound’s release date on Nintendo Switch 2 and Switch is now set. Today, it was revealed that the game will be out for both platforms on August 22, 2026. text:
Bunraku announced for Nintendo Switch 2

Efecto Studios has announced Bunraku for Nintendo Switch 2. The frantic platform brawler, inspired by the traditional art of Japanese puppet theater, is currently in development for Nintendo’s new console. A release window has not yet been announced. Bunraku is a fast-paced multiplayer brawler in which players clash in chaotic ninja battles. Up to eight players can compete in the...

text:

Right now you could argue that, in a way, 2026 is the year of Rayman. Ubisoft hadn’t done a ton with the franchise as of late, but that’s finally starting to change.
